questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A body is projected at an angle of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo>°</mo></math> with the horizontal such that the vertical component of its initial velocity is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>40</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The magnitude of velocity of the projectile at one quarter of its time of flight is nearly (Acceleration due to gravity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>)
questions.solution:
Suppose initial velocity is v and angle of projectionθ is given 60°.

Given,

Vertical component of initial velocity v sin θ=40 m s-1

⇒  v=46.19 m s-1

Time of flight T=2 v sin θg=2×4010=8 s

Given, time t=T4=2 s

Suppose vertical component of velocity after time t is v1.

Applying first eqaution of motion-

v1=v sin 60°-gt

⇒  v1=40-20=20 m s-1

Horizontal component remains uncharged.

∴  vH=v cos 60°=23.09 m s-1

Magnitude of velocity after time t vt=v12+vH2

⇒  vt=202+23.092=30.54 m s-1
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A particle P is projected from a point on the surface of a smooth inclined plane (see figure). Simultaneously another particle Q is released on the smooth inclined plane from the same position. P and Q collide on the inclined plane after t=4 seconds. Find the speed of projection in m/s .
questions.solution:
It can be observed from figure that P and Q shall collide if the initial component of velocity of P and Q on inclined plane i.e along incline should be equal. That is particle is projected perpendicular to incline.           



∴   Time of flight T = 2 u ⊥  g  cos  θ = 2 u  g  cos  θ

∴   u = g T  cos  θ 2 = 1 0  m / s
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A fighter plane, flying horizontally with a speed <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>360</mn><mo> </mo><mi>km</mi><mo>/</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></math> at an altitude of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>500</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> drops a bomb for a target straight ahead of it on the ground. The bomb should be dropped at what approximate distance ahead of the target? Assume that acceleration due to gravity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi>g</mi></mfenced></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><msup><mi>ms</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>. Also, neglect air drag.
questions.solution:
Given, speed of fighter plane, v=360 km/h

=360×518 m/s=100 m/s

Altitude, h=500 m

Now, from equation of motion,
h=ut+12gt2

⇒  500=0t+12×10×t2  ∵u=0 and g=10 m/s2

⇒  t2=100⇒t=10 s
∴ The bomb should be dropped at the distance,
x=vt=100×10=1000 m
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The horizontal and vertical displacements of a projectile at time $t$ are $x=36 t$ and $y=48 t-4.9 t^2$, respectively. Initial velocity of the projectile in $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$ is
questions.solution:
$$
x=36 t
$$
$$
y=48 t-4.9 t^2
$$
Comparing with $x=u \cos \theta t$
$$
y=u \sin \theta t-\frac{1}{2} g t^2
$$
where, $u=$ initial velocity
$$
\begin{aligned}
& u \cos \theta=36, \\
& u \sin \theta=48
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\begin{aligned}
(u \cos \theta)^2+(u \sin \theta)^2 & =36^2+48^2 \\
u^2\left(\cos ^2 \theta+\sin ^2 \theta\right) & =3600 \\
u^2 & =3600 \\
u & =60 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A wheel has a speed of 1200 revolutions per minute and is made to slow down at a rate of $4 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$. The number of revolutions it makes before coming to rest is
questions.solution:
$\omega^{2}=\omega_{0}^{2}-2 a \theta$
$$
\begin{aligned}
0 &=4 \pi^{2} n^{2}-2 a \theta \\
\theta &=\frac{4 \pi^{2}\left(\frac{1200}{60}\right)^{2}}{2 \times 4} \\
&=200 \pi^{2} \mathrm{rad} \\
\therefore \quad 2 \pi n &=200 \pi^{2} \\
n &=100 \pi=100 \times 3.14
\end{aligned}
$$
$=314$ revolution
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The horizontal and vertical displacements $x$ and $y$ of a projectile at a given time $t$ are given by $x=6 t$ metre and $y=8 t-5 t^2$ metre. The range of the projectile in metre is
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& x=(u \cos \theta) t=6 t \\
& \quad y=(u \sin \theta) t-\frac{1}{2} g t^2=8 t-5 t^2
\end{aligned}
$$
Therefore, $u \sin \theta=8$
$u \cos \theta=6$ 
$\begin{aligned} Range\ R & =\frac{u^2 \sin 2 \theta}{g}=\frac{u^2 \times 2 \sin \theta \cos \theta}{g} \\ & =\frac{2(u \sin \theta)(u \cos \theta)}{g} \\ & =\frac{2(8)(6)}{10}=9.6 \mathrm{~m}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A stone is thrown at <math><mn>25 </mn><mrow><mrow><mtext>m</mtext></mrow><mo>/</mo><mrow><mtext>s</mtext></mrow></mrow></math> at <math><msup><mrow><mn>53</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>o</mi></mrow></msup></math> above the horizontal. At what time its velocity is at <math><msup><mrow><mn>45</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>o</mi></mrow></msup></math> below the horizontal?
questions.solution:
Horizontal component of velocity, throughout the motion remain constant

using ucos θ=vcos α

25cos⁡53°=vcos⁡45°

⟹v=25×35.2

=152

⟹vy=vsin⁡45=15

Now using    Vy=uy+ayt in  y- direction,

-15=25sin⁡53°-gt

-15=25×45-10 t

⟹t=3.5 sec
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The real force ‘ <math><mi>F</mi></math> ’ acting on a particle of mass ‘ <math><mi>m</mi></math> ’ performing circular motion acts along the radius of circle ‘ <math><mi>r</mi></math> ’ and is directed towards the centre of circle. The square root of magnitude of such force is <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>T</mi><mo>=</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>
questions.solution:
F=mω2r
F=m2πT2.r
F=4π2T2.mr
⇒F=2πTmr
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A man standing on a road has to hold his umbrella at $30^{\circ}$ with the vertical to keep the rain away. He throws the umbrella and starts running at $10 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$. He finds that raindrops are hitting his head vertically. The actual speed of raindrops is :
questions.solution:
When the man is at rest with respect to the ground, the rain comes to him at an angle $30^{\circ}$ with the vertical. This is the direction of the velocity of raindrops with respect to the ground.
Here, $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{v}}_{r, g}=$ velocity of the rain with respect to the ground $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{v}}_{m, g}=$ velocity of the man with respect to the ground and $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{v}}_{r, m}=$ velocity of the rain with respect to the man. We have, $\quad \overrightarrow{\mathbf{v}}_{r, g}+\overrightarrow{\mathbf{v}}_{r, m}+\overrightarrow{\mathbf{v}}_{m, g}$ $\ldots$ (i) Taking horizontal components, Eq. (i) gives $v_{r, g} \sin 30^{\circ}=v_{m, g}=10 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$ or $\quad v_{r, g}=\frac{10}{\sin 30^{\circ}}=20 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A projectile is launched at time t=0 from point A which is at height 1 m above the floor with speed v m s-1 and at an angle θ=45° with the floor. It passes through a hoop at B which is 1 m above A and B is the highest point of the trajectory. The horizontal distance between A and B is d meters. The projectile then falls into a basket, hitting the floor at C a horizontal distance 3d meters from A. Find l (in m).
questions.solution:
The horizontal and vertical components of the velocity are the same, let it be u

So u=vx=vy=vcos45°.

From A to B:

H=vy22g⇒1=u22g⇒u2=2g

At B: d=ut1⇒t1=d/u

Equation of motion along vertical

Sy=uyt+12at2

⇒1=ut1-g2t12⇒1=u du-g2d2u2

⇒1=d-g2d2u2   ∵u2=2g

⇒1=d-gd24g⇒4=4d-d2

⇒d2-4d+4=0⇒d=2 m

At C: 3d=ut2⇒t2=3du

Equation of motion along vertical

Sy=uyt+12at2

-l=ut2-12gt22

⇒-l=u·3d4-g29d242=3d-9gd24g

⇒-l=3d-9d24=3×2-94×4=6-9=-3

⇒l=3 m
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A projectile is thrown with an initial velocity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>v</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math>. If the range of projectile is double the maximum height reached by it. Find the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>b</mi><mi>a</mi></mfrac></math>.
questions.solution:
Angle of projection, θ=tan-1 νyvx=tan-1ba

∴ tan θ=ba ...i

From formula: R=4H cot θ=2H

⇒cot θ=12

∴tan θ=2 As R=2H given ...ii

From equation i and ii, b=2a

ba=2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A ball is projected from the bottom of an inclined plane of inclination <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>30</mn><mi>o </mi></msup></math>, with a velocity of <math><mn>30 </mn><mi mathvariant="normal">m </mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>, at an angle of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>30</mn><mi>o</mi></msup></math> with the inclined plane. If  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10 </mn><msup><mi>ms</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>, then the range of the ball on given inclined plane is
questions.solution:
Range on incline plane,
  R = 2 u 2 sin α - β cos α g cos 2 β
Here,   β = 30 ο  and  α = 30 ο + 30 ο = 60 ο

 R= 2 ( 30 ) 2 sin( 30° )cos( 60° ) 10 cos 2  ( 30° )
    = 2 ( 30 ) 2 ( 1 2 )( 1 2 ) 10( 3 4 )
    = 60 m
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
An object of mass $2 m$ is projected with a speed of $100 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ at an angle $\theta=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{5}\right)$ to the horizontal. At the highest point, the object breaks into two pieces of same mass $m$ and the first one comes to rest. The distance between the point of projection and the point of landing of the bigger piece (in metre) is
(Given, $g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ )
questions.solution:
Horizontal range of the object fired,
$R=\frac{u^2 \sin 2 \theta}{g}$
At the highest point, when object is exploded into two equal masses, then
$2 m u \cos \theta=m(0)+m v$
or
$v=2 u \cos \theta$
It means, the horizontal velocity becomes double at the highest point, hence it will cover double the distance during the remaining flight.
$\therefore$ Total horizontal range of the other part
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{R}{2}+R=\frac{3 R}{2} \\ & =\frac{3}{2} \frac{u^2 \sin 2 \theta}{g}=\frac{3}{2} \times \frac{(100)^2 \times 2 \sin \theta \cos \theta}{g} \\ & =\frac{3}{2} \times \frac{(100)^2 \times 2 \times \frac{3}{5} \times \frac{4}{5}}{10}=1440 \mathrm{~m}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A body is projected vertically upwards at time $t=0$ and it is seen at a height $H$ at time $t_1$ and $t_2$ second during its flight. The maximum height attained is ( $g$ is acceleration due to gravity)
questions.solution:
Let time taken by the body to fal from point $C$ to $B$ is $t^{\prime}$. Then $t_1+2 t^{\prime}=t_2$
$t^{\prime}=\left(\frac{t_2-t_1}{2}\right) \ldots(\mathrm{i})$ Total time taken to reach point $C$ $\begin{aligned} T & =t_1+t^{\prime} \\ & =t_1+\frac{t_2-t_1}{2} \\ & =\frac{2 t_1+t_2-t_1}{2} \\ & =\left(\frac{t_1+t_2}{2}\right) \end{aligned}$ Maximum height attained $\begin{aligned} H_{\max } & =\frac{1}{2} g(T)^2 \\ & =\frac{1}{2} g\left(\frac{t_1+t_2}{2}\right)^2 \\ & =\frac{1}{2} g \cdot \frac{\left(t_1+t_2\right)^2}{4} \\ \Rightarrow \quad H_{\max } & =\frac{1}{8} g \cdot\left(t_1+t_2\right)^2 \mathrm{~m} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A body is projected from the top of a tower with a velocity $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{u}}=3 \hat{\mathbf{i}}+4 \hat{\mathbf{j}}+5 \hat{\mathbf{k}} \mathrm{ms}^{-1}$, where $\hat{\mathbf{i}}, \hat{\mathbf{j}}$ and $\hat{\mathbf{k}}$ are unit vectors along east, north and vertically upwards respectively. If the height of the tower is $30 \mathrm{~m}$, horizontal range of the body on the ground is $\left(g=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}\right)$
questions.solution:
( $\hat{\mathbf{k}}$ is given vertically upward direction) $$ \begin{aligned} & a=-10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2 \\ & h=-30 \mathrm{~m} \end{aligned} $$ As, $S=u t+\frac{1}{2} a t^2 \Rightarrow-30=5 t-\frac{1}{2} \times 10 \times t^2$ $$ \Rightarrow t^2-t-6=0 $$ $$ \Rightarrow t=-2 s \text { (Not possible) } $$ or, $t=32 \mathrm{~s}$. This is time in which body reaches the ground In this time, projectile moving in East and North with speeds $3 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ and $4 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. Distances covered in these directions are; In east $(x$ - coordinate $)=3 \times 3=9 \mathrm{~m}$ and in North $(y$ - coordinate $)=4 \times 3=12 \mathrm{~m}$ So, Projectile land at $(x, y) \equiv(9 \mathrm{~m}, 12 \mathrm{~m})$ mark. So, horizontal range of body on ground is:
Range $=\sqrt{9^2+12^2}=15 \mathrm{~m}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A particle at a height <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>h</mi></math> from the ground is projected with an angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn><mo>°</mo></math> from the horizontal. It strikes the ground making an angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>45</mn><mo>°</mo></math> with the horizontal. It is again projected from the same point with the same speed, but with an angle of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo>°</mo></math> with the horizontal. Find the angle it makes with the horizontal when it strikes the ground.
questions.solution:
In a projectile, the horizontal component of velocity remains constant. vx=ux=u cos30°=32u

Here, x and y are the components of velocity at the time it strikes the surface must be equal, because it strikes the surface at an angle of 45°.

 ∴vy=32u

So, v=vx2+vy2=32u

We know that, v is independent of the angle of projection.

So, the velocity with which it strikes the surface for the second case remains v=32u.

Let, it strike at angle ϕ, then,
vcosϕ=vx
vcosϕ=ucos60°
32ucosϕ=ucos60°
cosϕ=16 ⇒tanϕ=5
Hence, ϕ=tan-1(5).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A body of mass $m$ thrown up vertically with velocity $v_1$ reaches a maximum height $h_1$ in $t_1$ seconds. Another body of mass $2 \mathrm{~m}$ is projected with a velocity $v_2$ at an angle $\theta$. The second body reaches a maximum height $h_2$ in time $t_2$ seconds. If $t_1=2 t_2$, then ratio $\left(\frac{h_1}{h_2}\right)$ is
questions.solution:
Maximum vertical height attained by body thrown with velocity $v_1$
$$
h_1=\frac{v_1^2}{2 g}
$$
Another body of mass $2 \mathrm{~m}$ is projected with a velocity $v_2$ at an angle $\theta$.

$\therefore$ Height attained $\left(h_2\right)=\frac{v_2^2 \sin ^2 \theta}{2 g}$
$\therefore \quad \frac{h_1}{h_2}=\frac{v_1^2}{v_2^2} \sin ^2 \theta$
But $\quad t_1=2 t_2$
$\therefore \quad \frac{V_2}{g}=2\left[\frac{V_2 \sin \theta}{g}\right]$
$\Rightarrow \quad v_1=2 v_2 \sin \theta$
$\therefore \quad \frac{h_1}{h_2}=\left(\frac{2}{1}\right)^2=\frac{4}{1}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A man running at a speed of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>km</mi><mo>/</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></math> finds that the rain is falling vertically. When he stops running, he finds that the rain is falling at an angle of <math><msup><mrow><mn>60</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>o</mi></mrow></msup></math> with the horizontal. The velocity of rain with respect to running man is
questions.solution:
V r = velocity of rain   V rm = velocity of rain with respect to man V m = velocity of man


tan⁡30o=VmVrm

Vrm=513=53 km/h
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A small object is thrown at an angle $45^{\circ}$ to the horizontal with an initial velocity $\mathbf{v}_0$. The velocity is averaged for first $\sqrt{2} \mathrm{~s}$ and the magnitude of average velocity comes out to be same as that of initial velocity, i.e. $\left|\mathbf{v}_{\mathrm{o}}\right|$. The magnitude $\left|\mathbf{v}_0\right|$ will be (take, $g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ )
questions.solution: Let object is at $B(x, y)$ after $t=\sqrt{2} \mathrm{~s}$ Then, $x=u_x \times t=v_0 \cos 45^{\circ} \times \sqrt{2}=v_0$ and $y=u_y t-\frac{1}{2} a_y t^2=v_0\left(\sin 45^{\circ}\right) \sqrt{2}-\frac{1}{2}(10) \times(\sqrt{2})^2$ $$ =\left(v_0-10\right) \mathrm{m} $$ Displacement $O B$ of particle is $$ O B=\sqrt{O A^2+A B^2}=\sqrt{v_0^2+\left(v_0-10\right)^2} $$ So, $\quad v_{\text {avg }}=\frac{O B}{t}=v_0$ or $O B=v_0 t$ $$ \begin{array}{lrl} \Rightarrow & \sqrt{v_0^2+\left(v_0-10\right)^2}=v_0 \sqrt{2} & \Rightarrow v_0^2+\left(v_0-10\right)^2=2 v_0^2 \\ \Rightarrow & v_0-10= \pm v_0 & 2 v_0=10 \Rightarrow v_0=5 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1} \end{array} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A plane is inclined at an angle α=30° with respect to the horizontal. A particle is projected with a speed u=2 m s-1, from the base of the plane, making an angle θ=15° with respect to the plane as shown in the figure. The distance from the base, at which the particle hits the plane is close to [Take, g=10 m s-2]
questions.solution:
The given projectile is projected at an angle θ with an inclined plane making angle α with the horizontal. The net horizontal displacement of the particle as

R=uxT+12axT2       ...1

where, T= time of flight. Using formula for time of flight using a coordinate axis along the plane, we have

T=2uyay=2usinθgcosα     ...2

From equation 1 and 2, we have the range up the inclined plane is

R=ucosθ2usinθgcosα-12gsinα2usinθgcosα2    ...3

Here, u=2 m s-1, g=10 m s-2, θ=15°, α=30°

Substituting in equation 2,

T=2usinθgcosα=2×2sin15°10×cos30°

=2×2×0.258×210×1732=0.1191

Now, substituting values in equation 3R=2×cos15°×0.1191-12×10sin30°0.11912

=2×0.965×0.1191-520.11912

=0.229-0.0354=0.1936 m≈0.20 m=20 cm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The equation for the trajectory of a projectile is $y=\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{3}}-\frac{x^2}{60}\right) m$. The velocity of projection of the projectile is (Acceleration due to gravity $=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}$ )
questions.solution:
Coefficient of $x^2$ in equation of trajectory
$$
=\frac{\mathrm{g}}{2 \mathrm{u}^2 \cos ^2 \theta}
$$
So, $\frac{\mathrm{g}}{2 \mathrm{u}^2 \cos ^2 \theta}=\frac{1}{60} \Rightarrow \frac{10}{2 \mathrm{u}^2 \cos ^2 \theta}=\frac{1}{60}$
$$
\Rightarrow \mathrm{u}^2 \cos ^2 \theta=300
$$
Coefficient of $x$ in equation of trajectory is $\tan \theta$
So, $\tan \theta=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}$
$$
\Rightarrow \theta=30^{\circ}
$$
As $u^2 \cos ^2 \theta=300$
$$
\Rightarrow \mathrm{u}^2\left(\frac{3}{4}\right)=300
$$
$$
\Rightarrow \mathrm{u}^2=400
$$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{u}=20 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Three particles are projected in the air with the minimum possible speeds, such that the first goes from A to B, the second goes from B to C and the third goes from C to A. Points A and C are at the same horizontal level. The two inclines make the same angle α with the horizontal, as shown. The relation among the projection speeds of the three particles is
questions.solution:
For a given speed,

the maximum range of a projectile on a horizontal level is

Rmax=u2g

the maximum range up and down the incline are

Rupmax=u2g1+sinαRdownmax=u2g1-sinα

by substituting the values of the velocities given in the problem, we get

R=u22g1+sin⁡α⇒u22=Rg1+sin⁡α  …(1)
2Rcos⁡α= u32g⇒u32 =2Rgcos⁡α  …(2)
Now, u1u2=Rg1-sin2⁡α=Rg cos α=u322
∴u32 =2u1u2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A ball of mass $0.2 \mathrm{~kg}$ is thrown from a height of $1 \mathrm{~m}$ and with an initial velocity of $\sqrt{10} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ at an angle of $45^{\circ}$ with the horizontal. Assuming, acceleration due to gravity $g=10 \mathrm{~m} /$ $\mathrm{s}^2$, then modulus of momentum increment during the total time of motion in $\mathrm{kg} \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$ is
questions.solution:
There is no change in velocity in $x$-direction, so momentum change occurs only in $y$-direction.
Now initial momentum in $y$ direction is $$ \mathrm{p}_y(\text { initial })=m v_y=0.2\left(\sqrt{10} \sin 45^{\circ} \hat{\mathbf{j}}\right) $$ $$ =\frac{\mathrm{l}}{\sqrt{5}} \cdot \hat{\mathrm{j}} \mathrm{kg}^{-} \mathrm{ms}^{-1} $$ Final momentum in $y$-direction is $$ \begin{aligned} p y_{\text {final }} & =m v_y=m\left(\sqrt{\left.u_y^2+2 g h\right)}\right. \\ & =\left(0.2 \sqrt{\left(\sqrt{5}^2\right)+2(-10)(-1)}\right) \hat{\mathrm{j}} \\ & =-1 \hat{\mathrm{j}} \mathrm{kg}-\mathrm{ms}^{-1} \text { (downwards) } \end{aligned} $$ So, change in momentum $$ \begin{aligned} & \Delta p=p y_{(\text {fimal }\}}-p x_{\{\text {initial }\}}=-1 \hat{\mathbf{j}}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}} \hat{\mathrm{j}} \\ & =-\left(\mathrm{l}+\frac{\mathrm{l}}{\sqrt{5}}\right) \hat{\mathrm{j}} \mathrm{kg}^{-} \mathrm{ms}^{-1} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad=|\Delta p|=1+\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}=\frac{1+\sqrt{5}}{\sqrt{5}} \\ & \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: On an inclined plane, two particles A and B are projected with the same speed at the same angle with the horizontal, particle A down and particle B up the plane. If the ratio of time of flight of A and B is cotθ, where θ is the angle at which B is projected as measured from inclined plane, find the angle with horizontal at which particles are projected.
questions.solution:
Let, α be the angle of projection of B and β be the angle of elevation of the inclined plane as shown in the diagram below and tx denote the time period of particle x. Then,


 tA=2u sinα+βg cosβ and tB=2u sin(α-β) g cosβ

tAtB=sin(α+β)sin(α-β)

As given in the question, θ is the angle at which the particle B is projected as measured from the inclined plane, that is,

α-β =θ

tAtB=sin(α+β)sin(α-β)= cosθsinθ

sin(α+β)sin(α-β)= sin90°-θsinθ

α+β=90°-θ...1

α-β=θ...2

On solving equations 1 and 2, we get,

α = 45°
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A particle $A$ moves along the line, $y=30 \mathrm{~m}$ with a constant velocity, $\mathbf{v}$ parallel to $x$-axis. At the moment particle $A$ passes the $y$-axis, a particle $B$ starts from the origin with zero initial speed and a constant acceleration, $\mathbf{a}=0.40 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}^2$. The angle between $\mathbf{a}$ and $y$-axis is $60^{\circ}$. If the particles $A$ and $B$ collide after sometimes, then the value of $|\mathbf{v}|$ will be
questions.solution:
As shown in the figure, two particles are moving with velocity $v$ and acceleration a.
Let the time of collision of two particles be $t$, then the equation of motion, $$ s=u t+\frac{1}{2} a t^2 $$ For particle $A$, $$ \begin{aligned} 30 \mathrm{~m} & =(0) t+\frac{a \cos 60^{\circ} t^2}{2} \\ \Rightarrow \quad 30 & =\frac{0.4}{2} \times \frac{1}{2} \times t^2 \\ t & =\sqrt{300} \end{aligned} $$ Hence, in time $t=\sqrt{300}$, both travelled equal distance in horizontal direction, so that the collision takes place. $$ \Rightarrow u t=\frac{1}{2} a \cos 30^{\circ} t^2\left(\because 30^{\circ}=90^{\circ}-60^{\circ}\right) $$ $$ \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad u=\frac{1}{2} \times 0.4 \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \times \sqrt{300} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad u=3 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \\ & \end{aligned} $$ Hence, the correct option is (2).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
A plane is inclined at an angle α=30° with respect to the horizontal. A particle is projected with a speed  u=2 m s-1 , from the base of the plane, making an angle θ=15° with respect to the plane as shown in the figure. The distance from the base, at which the particle hits the plane is close to:
(Take  g=10 m s-2 )
questions.solution:
Range =2u2sinαcos⁡α+θgcos2⁡θ
α=angle of inclination=30°
θ=angle of projection from inclination=15°
u=2 m/s
R=222sin15°cos⁡15°+30°gcos2⁡30°=8sin⁡15°cos45°g cos230°  sin15°=3-122
R=453-1 3≈0.2 m
R≈20 cm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two towers <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math>, each of height <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> are situated a distance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>200</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> apart. A body thrown horizontally from the top of the tower A with a velocity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><msup><mi>ms</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> towards the tower <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> hits the ground at point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> and another body thrown horizontally from the top of tower <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> with a velocity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn><mo> </mo><msup><mi>ms</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> towards the tower A hits the ground at point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi></math>. If a car starting from rest from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> reaches <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> seconds, the acceleration of the car is _________ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo></math>Acceleration due to gravity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><msup><mi>ms</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>)</mo></math>
questions.solution:
The time taken by both the ball to reach the ground is same because initial vertical velocity and height of the tower is the same in each case,

AP=20×2hg=20×4010=40 mQB=20×2hg=30×4010=60 m

PQ=200-100=ut+12at2=0+12×a100100=50aa=2 m s-2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If a stone is to hit at a point which is at a horizontal distance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> away and at a height <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> above the point from where the stone starts, then what is the value of initial speed <math><mi>u</mi></math> if the stone is launched at an angle <math><mn>45</mn><mo>°</mo></math>?
questions.solution:
From equation of trajectory,
y=xtan⁡θ-gx22y2cos2⁡θ
⇒50=100tan⁡45°-1010022u2cos2⁡45°
⇒u2=10025
⇒u=205 m/s
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A ball is projected with a velocity u at a right angle to the inclined plane which is inclined at an angle α with the horizontal. The distance x along the inclined plane that it will travel before again striking the slope is
questions.solution:
It is given that a ball is projected perpendicular to an inclined plane.

u=Initial velocity

α=Angle of elevation of the slope

Acceleration due to gravity is vertically downward, and can be resolved into a perpendicular component and a parallel component to the plane as gcosα and gsinα.

Equation of motion perpendicular to the incline is

⇒y=ut-12gcosαt2
Upon striking the plane, the net displacement perpendicular to the incline is zero, i.e, y=0.
⇒t=2ugcosα

We need to find the distance travelled by the ball along the plane during this time.

Applying equation of motion along the plane,

x =0+12gsinαt2=12gsinα2ugcosα2=2u2gtanαcosα
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A body of mass ' $\mathrm{m}$ ' is moving with speed ' $\mathrm{V}$ ' along a circular path of radius ' $r$ '. Now the speed is reduced to $\frac{\mathrm{V}}{2}$ and radius is increased to ' $3 r$ '. For this change, initial centripetal force needs to be
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Centripetal force, } \mathrm{F}=\frac{\mathrm{mv}^2}{\mathrm{r}} \\ & \mathrm{F}_1=\frac{\mathrm{mv}_1^2}{\mathrm{r}_1} \text { and } \mathrm{F}_2=\frac{\mathrm{mv}_2^2}{\mathrm{r}_2} \\ & \frac{\mathrm{F}_2}{\mathrm{~F}_1}=\frac{\mathrm{v}_2^2}{\mathrm{v}_1^2} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{r}_1}{\mathrm{r}_2} \\ & \mathrm{v}_2=\frac{\mathrm{v}_1}{2} \text { and } \mathrm{r}_2=3 \mathrm{r}_1 \\ & \frac{\mathrm{F}_2}{\mathrm{~F}_1}=\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^2 \cdot \frac{1}{3}=\frac{1}{12} \\ & \mathrm{v}_2=\frac{\mathrm{v}_1}{2} \text { and } \mathrm{r}_2=3 \mathrm{r}_1 \\ & \frac{\mathrm{F}_2}{\mathrm{~F}_1}=\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^2 \cdot \frac{1}{3}=\frac{1}{12} \\ & \mathrm{~F}_2=\frac{\mathrm{F}_1}{12}\end{aligned}$
Change in force $=F_2-F_1=\frac{F_1}{12}-F_1=-\frac{11}{12} F_1$ Hence force is decreased by $\frac{11}{12}$ times.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A particle of mass $10 \mathrm{~g}$ moves along a circle of radius $6.4 \mathrm{~cm}$ with a constant tangential acceleration. If the kinetic energy of the particle becomes $8 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~J}$ by the end of the second revolution after the beginning of the motion, the magnitude of the tangential acceleration is
questions.solution:
Consider the following diagram:
The tangential acceleration is given by, $\begin{aligned} & a_t=\frac{\mathrm{d} v}{\mathrm{~d} t}=\text { Constant } \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{d} v}{\mathrm{~d} t}=a_t \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{d} s}{\mathrm{~d} t} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d} v}{\mathrm{~d} s}=a t \\ & \Rightarrow v=\frac{\mathrm{d} v}{\mathrm{~d} t}=a_t \\ & \Rightarrow \int_0^v \frac{v^2}{2} \mathrm{~d} v=\int_0^{(4 \pi r)} a_t \mathrm{~d} s \end{aligned}$ We know,
Now, kinetic energy is given by, $K E=\frac{m v^2}{2}$
Now, using eq ${ }^{\mathrm{n}}(1) \&(2)$ $\begin{aligned} & \left(\frac{2 K E}{m}\right)=(8 \pi r) a_t \\ & \Rightarrow a_t=\left(\frac{K E}{(4 \pi r) m}\right) \end{aligned}$ Given, $K E=8 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~J}, r=6.4 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~m}$ and $m=1 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~kg}$. $\Rightarrow a_t=\frac{\left(8 \times 10^{-4}\right) \mathrm{J}}{4 \pi\left(6.4 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~m}\right)\left(10^{-2} \mathrm{~kg}\right)}=0.1 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A particle is projected from a stationary trolley. After projection, the trolley moves with a velocity 215 m s-1. For an observer on the trolley, the direction of the particle is as shown in .the figure while for the observer on the ground, the ball rises vertically. The maximum height reached by the ball from the trolley is h meter. The value of h will be ______.
questions.solution:
Given that

Velocity of trolley  u=215 m s-1.

  

For an observer on the ground, the ball rises vertically

So velocity of ball in horizontal direction will be zero with respect ground.

vx=0⇒vcos60°-u=0⇒vcos60°=215 

⇒v=415 m s-1

Vertical component of velocity of ball

∴vy=vsin60°=41532=245 ms-1

Maximum height of ball from ground is

Hmax=vy22g=4×4520=9 m
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
A mass 'm' is tied to one end of a spring and whirled in a horizontal circle with
constant angular velocity. The elongation in the spring is $1 \mathrm{~cm} .$ If the angular speed
is doubled, the elongation in the spring is $6 \mathrm{~cm}$. The original length of the spring is
questions.solution:
Let $\ell$ be the original length of the spring.
Let the initial angular velocity be $\omega$ and the corresponding elongation $\mathbf{e}_{1}=1 \mathrm{~cm}$.
When the angular velocity is doubled the elongation $e_{2}=6 \mathrm{~cm}$.
If $k$ is the spring constant then we have
$$
\begin{array}{l}
\mathrm{m}\left(\ell+e_{1}\right) \omega^{2}=\mathrm{ke}_{1} \\
\text { and } \mathrm{m}\left(\ell+e_{2}\right)(2 \omega)^{2}=\mathrm{ke}_{2} \\
\text { or } \quad \mathrm{m}\left(\ell+e_{2}\right) \cdot 4 \omega^{2}=\mathrm{ke}_{2}
\end{array}
$$
Dividing Eq(1) by Eq(2), we get
$$
\frac{\ell+e_{1}}{4\left(\ell+e_{2}\right)}=\frac{e_{1}}{e_{2}}
$$
solving we get $\ell=9 \mathrm{~cm}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An archer shoots an arrow from a height $4.2 \mathrm{~m}$ above the ground with a speed $40 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ and at an angle $30^{\circ}$ as shown in the figure. Determine the horizontal distance $R$ covered by the arrow, when it hits the ground, (Take $g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ )
questions.solution:
Given, speed of arrow, $v=40 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ and $\theta=30^{\circ}$
Horizontal range $R_1$ covered by the arrow is given by $\begin{aligned} R_1 & =\frac{v^2 \sin 2 \theta}{g} \\ & =\frac{40^2 \sin (2 \times 30)}{10}=160 \sin 60^{\circ} \\ & =160 \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}=80 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~m}\end{aligned}$ If $t$ be the time taken by the arrow reaching from $B$ to $C$, then from the second equation of motion, $\Rightarrow \quad h=u t+\frac{1}{2} g t^2$ here, $h=4.2 \mathrm{~m}, u=v \sin 30^{\circ}$ $4.2=v \sin 30^{\circ} t+\frac{1}{2} g t^2$ $4.2=40 \times \frac{1}{2} t+\frac{1}{2} \times 10 t^2$ $4.2=20 t+5 t^2$ $\begin{aligned} & 50 t^2+200 t-42=0 \\ & 25 t^2+100 t-21=0\end{aligned}$ Solving the quadratic equation, $t=\frac{1}{5} \mathrm{~s}$ Distance travelled in horizontal direction is given by $\begin{aligned} R_2 & =v \cos 30^{\circ} \times t \\ & =40 \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \times \frac{1}{5}=4 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~m}\end{aligned}$ Hence, the horizontal distance covered by the arrow, $\begin{aligned} R & =R_1+R_2 \\ & =80 \sqrt{3}+4 \sqrt{3}=84 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~m}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A ball is horizontally projected with a speed <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>v</mi></math> from the top of a plane inclined at an angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>45</mn><mo>°</mo></math> with the horizontal. How far from the point of projection will the ball strike the plane?
questions.solution:
From the above diagram, in ∆OAB,

tan45°=OAAB=yx

⇒y=x  ...1

From equation of motion along vertical direction, the displacement is given by,

y=uyT+12ayT2; here uy=0, T is time of flight and ay=g

y=0+12gT2  ...2

Along horizontal direction, there is no force or acceleration in this direction, so,

x=v×T  ...3

From equation 1, 2 and 3,

12gT2=v×T

T=2vg

Hence, we have

x=y=v×2vg=2v2g

Therefore, the distance moved on the inclined plane will be given by,

OB=x2+y2

OB=22v2g.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A ball of mass ' $\mathrm{m}$ ' is attached to the free end of a string of length ' $l$ '. The ball is moving in horizontal circular path about the vertical axis as shown in the diagram. The angular velocity ' $\omega$ ' of the ball will be [ $\mathrm{T}=$ Tension in the string. $]$
questions.solution: The tension in the string can be resolved in two components along the perpendicular axis. The gravitational force is acting downwards and the centrifugal force is acting in $-\mathrm{x}$ direction $$ \begin{aligned} & \mathrm{T} \sin \theta=\mathrm{mr} \omega^2 \\ \therefore \quad & \omega^2=\frac{\mathrm{T} \sin \theta}{\mathrm{mr}} \end{aligned} $$ $$ \therefore \quad \omega=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{T} \sin \theta}{\mathrm{mr}}} $$ From figure, $\sin \theta=\frac{\mathrm{r}}{l}$ $$ \begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad \omega=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{Tr}}{\mathrm{mr} l}} \\ & \therefore \quad \omega=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{T}}{\mathrm{m} l}} \\ & \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The equation of motion of a projectile are given by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>36</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi></math> metre and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>96</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>9</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>8</mn><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> metre. The angle of projection is
questions.solution:
x=36 t

∴   vx=dxdt=36 m s-1

y=48 t-4.9 t2

∴   vy=48-9.8 t

at t=0 and vy=48 m s-1

So, angle of projection

θ=tan-1 vyvx=tan-1 43

Or  θ=sin-1 (4/5)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A ball is thrown at $30^{\circ}$ with horizontal, from the top of roof $20 \mathrm{~m}$ high with a speed of $13 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$. At what distance from the throwing point will the ball, once again be at a height of $20 \mathrm{~m}$ from the ground? $\left(g=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}\right)$
questions.solution:
The situation is as shown below.
Given, $\theta=30^{\circ}$, initial speed, $u=13 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ $$ \begin{aligned} \text { Range } & =\frac{u^2 \sin 2 \theta}{g} \\ & =\frac{(13)^2 \times \sin 60^{\circ}}{10}=\frac{169 \times \sqrt{3}}{10 \times 2}=14.6 \mathrm{~m} \end{aligned} $$ $\therefore$ Range, $R=14.6 \mathrm{~m}$ Hence, the ball once again be at a height of $20 \mathrm{~m}$ from the ground at $14.6 \mathrm{~m}$ from the point of projection.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two cars $A$ and $B$ are moving with speeds $v_A=120 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$ and $v_B=50 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$ respectively in the directions as indicated by the arrow in the figure below. What is the relative speed of the car $B$ with respect to car $A$ ?
questions.solution:
The given situation is shown in the following figure
Speed of car $A$, $v_A=120 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h} \Rightarrow \mathbf{v}_A=-120 \hat{\mathbf{i}}$ Speed of car $B$, $v_B=50 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$ $\mathbf{v}_B=-50 \hat{\mathbf{j}}$ $\therefore$ Relative speed of the car $B$ w.r.t. car $A$, $\mathbf{v}_{B A}=\mathbf{v}_B-\mathbf{v}_A=-50 \hat{\mathbf{j}}-(-120 \hat{\mathbf{i}})$ $=-50 \hat{\mathbf{j}}+120 \hat{\mathbf{i}}=120 \hat{\mathbf{i}}-50 \hat{\mathbf{j}}$ $\therefore \quad\left|\mathbf{v}_{B A}\right|=\sqrt{(120)^2+(-50)^2}$ $=\sqrt{16900}=130 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An object is projected with a velocity of $20 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ making an angle of $45^{\circ}$ with horizontal. The equation for the trajectory is $h=A x-B x^2$ where $h$ is height, $x$ is horizontal distance $A$ and $B$ are constants. The ratio $A: B$ is $\left(g=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}\right)$
questions.solution:
$u=20 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}, \theta=45^{\circ}$
Equation of trajectory $h=A x-B x^2$ Standard equation
$h=x \tan \theta-\frac{g x^2}{2 u^2 \cos ^2 \theta}$
On comparing the two equations
$\begin{aligned} A & =\tan \theta, B=\frac{g}{2 u^2 \cos ^2 \theta} \\ \frac{A}{B} & =\frac{\tan \theta}{\frac{g}{2} u^2 \cos ^2 \theta}=\frac{2 u^2 \cos ^2 \theta \tan \theta}{g} \\ & =\frac{2 \times(20)^2 \cos ^2 45^{\circ} \tan 45^{\circ}}{10} \\ & =\frac{2 \times 400 \times\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^2 \times 1}{10} \\ & =2 \times 40 \times \frac{1}{2}=40\end{aligned}$
$A: B=40: 1$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
